ENTRY            32927   20251202                             32213292700000001 
SUBENT        32927001   20251202                             32213292700100001 
BIB                 12         43                                 3292700100002 
TITLE      New measurement of the neutron-induced total cross-    3292700100003 
            sections of natCr in a wide energy range on the       3292700100004 
            Back-n at CSNS                                        3292700100005 
AUTHOR     (Baoqian Li, Min Xiao, Yonghao Chen, Jieming Xue,      3292700100006 
            Xinxiang Li, Han Yi, Pinjing Cheng, Rong Liu,         3292700100007 
            Yiwei Yang, Zijie Han, Dajun Zhao, Haoqiang Wang,     3292700100008 
            Jirong Zhao, Peng Luan, Jing Liu, Zijun Liu,          3292700100009 
            Chaomin Chen, Wen Luo, Bo Zheng, Song Feng)           3292700100010 
INSTITUTE  (3CPRIHP,3CPRNPC)                                      3292700100011 
           (3CPRCPR) University of South China, Hengyang          3292700100012 
REFERENCE  (J,CPH/C,48,104002,2024)                               3292700100013 
           #doi:10.1088/1674-1137/ad66c0                          3292700100014 
FACILITY   (SYNCH,3CPRIHP) The back-streaming white neutron       3292700100015 
            beamline (Back-n) at the China Spallation Neutron     3292700100016 
            Source (CSNS)                                         3292700100017 
INC-SOURCE (SPALL) The proton beam current was in double-bunch    3292700100018 
            mode, the average proton hitting power was 125 kW.    3292700100019 
            In each pulse, there were two proton bunches          3292700100020 
            approximately 30 ns wide (rms) with a 410 ns interval.3292700100021 
SAMPLE     A 15 mm thick natural chromium sample, with purity of  3292700100022 
            99.99%, diameter of 70 mm, mass of 416.5 g and        3292700100023 
            density of 7.26 g/cm3.                                3292700100024 
DETECTOR   (FISCH) A multi-layer fast fission chamber consisted   3292700100025 
            of high purity fission cells, front and rear neutron  3292700100026 
            windows, and a drum cavity. Four cells of 235U and    3292700100027 
            four cells of 238U were arranged in the multi-layer   3292700100028 
            fast fission ionization chamber for low-energy and    3292700100029 
            fast-energy neutron detection, respectively. The time 3292700100030 
            resolution was better than 6 ns.                      3292700100031 
METHOD     (TOF,TRN) Neutron flight path of four different 235U   3292700100032 
            fission cells were 77.7380 m, 77.7566 m, 77.7768 m    3292700100033 
            and 77.7966 m. Because 238U fission cells are not     3292700100034 
            sensitive to low-energy neutrons, the flight          3292700100035 
            distances of four 238U fission cells were obtained by 3292700100036 
            increasing the 20 mm distance sequentially.           3292700100037 
ERR-ANALYS (DATA-ERR) The uncertainty primarily arises from the   3292700100038 
            atomic density of the sample, the statistical         3292700100039 
            uncertainty in neutron counting and the fission       3292700100040 
            events.                                               3292700100041 
STATUS     (TABLE,,Baoqian Li+,J,CPH/C,48,104002,2024)            3292700100042 
            Data presented by author                              3292700100043 
HISTORY    (20250110R) Data received from Song Feng               3292700100044 
           (20251202C) Jimin Wang                                 3292700100045 
ENDBIB              43          0                                 3292700100046 
COMMON               1          3                                 3292700100047 
THICKNESS 1                                                       3292700100048 
ATOMS/B                                                           3292700100049 
      0.125                                                       3292700100050 
ENDCOMMON            3          0                                 3292700100051 
ENDSUBENT           50          0                                 3292700199999

REACTION  1(24-CR-0(N,TOT),,TRN)                                  3292700200003 
          2(24-CR-0(N,TOT),,SIG)                                  3292700200004 
COMMENT    The measured results with 235U cells                   3292700200005 
STATUS     (COREL,32927003) Data measured with 238U cells         3292700200006
